div1.entryFree: Millstone, used only in the proverbial expression to weep --s == not to weep at all, to remain hard and unfeeling as a stone: your eyes drop --s, when fools' eyes drop tears, R3 I, 3, 354. he will weep. Ay, --s, I, 4, 246. Applied to tears of laughter: Queen Hecuba laughed that her eyes ran o'er. With --s, Troil. I, 2, 158.
